core.action: Weight each sector/group equally in group-neutral portfolios to prevent sector allocation decisions from contaminating factor performance attribution

consequence.description: Using market-cap or other unequal group weights reintroduces sector rotation effects into factor returns, making it impossible to isolate pure factor performance; backtest results conflate factor alpha with sector bets, causing live trading allocation errors

evidence_refs.summary: f0a07c2 Derived from BD: BD-034

core.when: When annualizing performance metrics for comparison across strategies with different observation frequencies
core.action: Use 252 trading days as the annualization factor for US equity strategies, converting daily/weekly returns to annual equivalents

consequence.description: Using different annualization factors (250, 260, 365) produces inconsistent annualized returns, making cross-strategy comparison unreliable; mixing factors in reporting causes misleading performance attribution

evidence_refs.summary: f0a07c2 Derived from BD: BD-035

core.when: When computing factor autocorrelation to measure factor ranking stability over time
core.action: Compute factor autocorrelation on rank-transformed values (not raw factor scores) to verify scale-invariant persistence measurement

consequence.description: Computing autocorrelation on raw factor values makes persistence measurements sensitive to factor scaling and outliers; two identical factors with different scales (0-1 vs 0-100) show different autocorrelation properties even though rankings are identical

evidence_refs.summary: f0a07c2 Derived from BD: BD-036

core.when: When converting rates of return across different time periods for strategy reporting
core.action: Verify that returns follow compound return properties (multiplicative chaining) before applying geometric mean conversion; do not apply geometric mean to arithmetic (non-compounding) return series

consequence.description: Geometric mean overstates performance when applied to non-compounding returns or short-horizon additive returns; strategy reporting using geometric mean for inappropriately additive return series produces misleading annual performance figures

evidence_refs.summary: f0a07c2 Derived from BD: BD-038

core.when: When comparing Information Coefficient (IC) metrics across different forward return horizons in factor research
core.action: Verify that IC comparisons use consistent annualization methodology — for short-horizon factors (1D-5D returns), recognize that annualization amplifies variance by sqrt(252) ≈ 16x, potentially making annualized IC appear 94% worse than daily IC for factors with realistic IC_mean=0.05 and IC_std=0.03

consequence.description: Comparing annualized IC across different horizons without normalization creates misleading impressions about factor quality, causing researchers to reject short-horizon factors that appear to have poor risk-adjusted IC when in fact they outperform long-horizon factors on a daily basis

evidence_refs.summary: f0a07c2 Derived from BD: BD-099

core.when: When implementing or modifying position PnL calculation logic in backtesting
core.action: Assume the framework automatically conserves total PnL across realized and unrealized components — the framework does not implement PnL conservation tracking between realized gains/losses from closed positions and unrealized mark-to-market values

consequence.description: Without PnL conservation, backtest results may show inconsistent total PnL that does not reconcile with individual trade records, causing audit failures and incorrect strategy performance reporting in production

evidence_refs.summary: f0a07c2 Derived from BD: BD-GAP-005

core.when: When calculating position PnL in backtesting
core.action: Implement explicit PnL conservation by tracking realized_pnl (from closed positions) and unrealized_pnl (mark-to-market), and verify total_pnl == realized_pnl + unrealized_pnl at each timestamp with tolerance < 1e-6

consequence.description: Without explicit PnL conservation tracking, discrepancies between realized and unrealized PnL remain undetected, causing audit failures when live trading records cannot be reconciled with backtest projections

evidence_refs.summary: f0a07c2 Derived from BD: BD-GAP-005

core.when: When implementing or modifying data annotation logic in backtesting
core.action: Assume the framework provides explicit currency and unit annotations for monetary values — the framework does not implement currency/unit metadata, treating each values as dimensionless numbers

consequence.description: Without explicit currency annotations, multi-currency portfolios mix values without conversion, causing systematic over/under-reporting of total portfolio value when assets are denominated in different currencies

evidence_refs.summary: f0a07c2 Derived from BD: BD-GAP-007

core.when: When annotating monetary values in backtest reports
core.action: Add explicit currency_code field (e.g., 'CNY', 'USD', 'HKD') to each monetary value annotations and verify that cross-currency aggregations apply proper exchange rate conversions before summing

consequence.description: Without explicit currency annotations, backtest reports cannot distinguish CNY from USD positions, causing incorrect portfolio totals when aggregating multi-currency holdings in cross-border trading strategies

evidence_refs.summary: f0a07c2 Derived from BD: BD-GAP-007

core.action: Verify that the fallback calendar (pd.tseries.offsets.BDay()) matches the actual exchange trading schedule — for A-shares, Hong Kong, or European markets, the standard business day calendar misaligns with actual trading days and holiday schedules

evidence_refs.summary: f0a07c2 Derived from BD: BD-042

core.when: When using Information Coefficient rolling average with default 22-day window
core.action: Verify that 22-day (approximately one trading month) window matches the intended analysis horizon — shorter windows (5-day) capture noise while longer windows (60-day) smooth out regime changes; adjust based on factor rebalance frequency

consequence.description: Using 22-day IC rolling window without considering factor rebalance frequency causes smoothed IC to misrepresent factor effectiveness, as the rolling average blends periods with different market regimes

evidence_refs.summary: f0a07c2 Derived from BD: BD-044

core.when: When implementing event study signal processing logic
core.action: Verify positive event values are interpreted as long-entry signals and negative event values are interpreted as short-entry signals — do not use magnitude-based event detection without explicit sign handling

consequence.description: Reversing the event sign convention causes the strategy to enter short positions when expecting long entries and vice versa, producing inverted returns that systematically lose money in the opposite direction of the intended event study

evidence_refs.summary: f0a07c2 Derived from BD: BD-045

core.when: When calculating factor portfolio positions and return attribution
core.action: Use active weights that track position entry and exit over time with proper signal expiry tracking — do not use static weights from formation date, as they misattribute returns when factor signals change

consequence.description: Using static weights instead of active weights misattributes returns to factors that have already exited positions, causing incorrect performance attribution and leading to retention of underperforming factor strategies

evidence_refs.summary: f0a07c2 Derived from BD: BD-047

core.when: When interpreting factor quantile results or constructing quantile-based portfolios
core.action: Use 1-indexed quantiles where quantile 1 represents lowest factor values and the highest number represents highest factor values — do not assume zero-indexed quantiles (0 to N-1) without explicit conversion

consequence.description: Using zero-indexed quantile interpretation inverts portfolio construction, causing the strategy to take long positions on the lowest factor quantiles and short positions on the highest quantiles, systematically reversing expected factor returns

evidence_refs.summary: f0a07c2 Derived from BD: BD-049

core.action: Assume the framework handles currency and denomination differences — the framework does not implement currency annotation on financial quantities; mixed-currency operations produce mathematically incorrect results without explicit currency tracking

evidence_refs.summary: f0a07c2 Derived from BD: BD-GAP-018

core.action: Add currency_code and denomination fields to each financial quantity fields (amount, value, price, return, cost), and implement validation that prevents mixed-currency operations or converts to a common base currency

consequence.description: Without explicit currency annotation, multi-currency portfolios aggregate incompatible values, leading to systematically wrong portfolio totals and incorrect strategy performance attribution

evidence_refs.summary: f0a07c2 Derived from BD: BD-GAP-018

core.action: Verify price data starts at factor index plus one row (iloc[1:]) to pair current factor signals with future price changes — using same-day pricing (iloc[0:]) introduces look-ahead bias

evidence_refs.summary: f0a07c2 Derived from BD: BD-060

core.action: Use pd.qcut for equal-frequency quantile bucketing based on empirical data percentiles — avoid pd.cut with fixed thresholds which creates unequal group sizes with real-world factor distributions

consequence.description: Using fixed-width bins instead of equal-frequency quantile buckets creates unbalanced group sizes, causing sparse edge buckets to distort mean-return comparisons and produce misleading factor rankings

evidence_refs.summary: f0a07c2 Derived from BD: BD-061

core.when: When applying Z-score outlier filtering to forward returns
core.action: Explicitly set and validate the filter_zscore parameter based on the expected return distribution — values too low lose valid data from extreme-but-real events, values too high preserve noise from corporate actions and data errors

consequence.description: Using an inappropriate Z-score threshold either removes legitimate extreme returns or fails to filter outliers from corporate actions, distorting quantile mean return calculations and compromising factor analysis validity

evidence_refs.summary: f0a07c2 Derived from BD: BD-063

core.when: When computing factor portfolio returns from weighted forward returns
core.action: Explicitly define and justify the weighting method (weighted sum vs equal weighting) based on whether factor signal strength is available — weighted sum produces factor-specific P&L, equal weighting ignores signal magnitude

consequence.description: Using equal-weighted returns instead of weighted sum fundamentally changes portfolio construction, ignoring factor signal strength and producing different P&L attribution that misrepresents factor effectiveness

evidence_refs.summary: f0a07c2 Derived from BD: BD-068

core.when: When annualizing factor returns using the compound geometric formula
core.action: Verify that returns are approximately i.i.d. before relying on annualized figures — volatile strategies with path dependency may have misleading annualized values that overstate true expected returns

consequence.description: Geometric annualization assumes i.i.d. returns, but volatile strategies exhibit serial correlation and path dependency, causing annualized figures to overstate expected performance and mislead strategy comparison

evidence_refs.summary: f0a07c2 Derived from BD: BD-070

core.when: When calculating standard error of quantile mean returns via SE = std/sqrt(n)
core.action: Verify return normality assumption before using classical SE formula — heavy-tailed distributions require bootstrap or robust SE methods; the classical formula underestimates uncertainty for fat-tailed returns

consequence.description: The classical SE formula assumes normally distributed returns, but return distributions typically exhibit heavy tails and skewness, causing underestimated confidence intervals and false statistical significance conclusions

evidence_refs.summary: f0a07c2 Derived from BD: BD-071

core.when: When implementing or refactoring spread standard error calculations in quantile performance analysis
core.action: Use sqrt(SE1² + SE2²) formula for joint standard error of quantile spread — this assumes independence between top and bottom quantile groups

consequence.description: Using incorrect SE formula or ignoring independence assumption causes misaligned statistical significance estimates, leading to trades being wrongly classified as significant or insignificant based on flawed confidence intervals

evidence_refs.summary: f0a07c2 Derived from BD: BD-072

core.when: When implementing cumulative return calculations for strategy comparison
core.action: Use empyrical.cum_returns with starting_value=1 to normalize strategies to a common 1.0 starting point, ensuring fair comparison across different capital bases

consequence.description: Using simple cumulative sum instead of properly compounded returns ignores the compounding effect, causing backtested results to diverge significantly from actual realized returns in live trading

evidence_refs.summary: f0a07c2 Derived from BD: BD-073

core.when: When computing risk-adjusted IC metrics for factor quality assessment
core.action: Calculate risk-adjusted IC as IC_mean divided by IC_std (Sharpe-like ratio), which annualizes consistency and captures both magnitude and stability of factor predictive power

consequence.description: Using raw IC_mean without normalization by volatility misleads factor quality assessment; a high mean but volatile IC may indicate an unreliable factor that appears strong on average but fails in live trading

evidence_refs.summary: f0a07c2 Derived from BD: BD-075

validation_threshold: IC_mean exists AND IC_std > 0 AND risk_adjusted_ic = IC_mean / IC_std → PASS; IC_std == 0 → FAIL (undefined)

core.when: When performing hypothesis testing on IC mean to determine factor statistical significance
core.action: Use one-sample t-test (ttest_1samp) to assess whether IC mean is statistically distinguishable from zero, accounting for IC time-series variability

consequence.description: Omitting t-test or using improper hypothesis testing causes inability to distinguish genuine factor predictive power from random noise, leading to deployment of factors with no real signal or abandonment of valid factors

evidence_refs.summary: f0a07c2 Derived from BD: BD-076
